nodes.title: Validate cache capacity early

anchor.snippet: maxSize must be a positive integer

nodes.why: Failing early avoids weird runtime behavior from invalid sizes.
nodes.alternative: Silently coerce invalid values (for example, floor to 1).
nodes.tradeoff: Strict validation is safer, but callers must pass correct input.

nodes.title: Use Map to keep insertion order
nodes.why: Map gives O(1)-style key lookups and stable key order for LRU logic.
nodes.alternative: Use a plain object plus a separate order array.
nodes.tradeoff: Map keeps logic simple, but needs modern JS runtime support.

nodes.title: Refresh recency with delete + set
nodes.why: Reinserting the key moves it to the most-recent end.
nodes.alternative: Track recency in a custom linked list.
nodes.tradeoff: Very compact approach, but tightly coupled to Map order semantics.

nodes.title: Evict from the oldest end
nodes.why: The first key in the map is the least recently used one.
nodes.alternative: Evict randomly or by simple FIFO queue.
nodes.tradeoff: True LRU improves hit quality, with a tiny extra maintenance step.

nodes.title: Make read access affect LRU order
nodes.why: In LRU, a successful read means that item is actively used.
nodes.tradeoff: More accurate LRU behavior, with a small extra write on each hit.

nodes.title: Write first, then enforce size
nodes.why: This keeps set() logic simple and guarantees capacity is respected.
nodes.alternative: Check fullness before set and branch for update/new key.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner control flow, but still performs eviction checks per set.
